subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF EFF program/Art IV, World Bank, ADB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, UN) + Pakistani official (PBS, State Bank of Pakistan) + Pakistani independent press (Dawn, The News, Express Tribune, Profit).
A distressed-but-stabilising economy under IMF tutelage, a military-dominant civilian hybrid with a legitimacy crisis, resurgent terrorism, and catastrophic climate exposure.

Standout drags: chronic debt distress, weak institutions, a manipulated-election legitimacy crisis, terrorism, and extreme flood/heat exposure. Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone; Urdu/regional reporting under-read.

economic.notes: A fragile IMF-dependent stabilisation, against chronic debt distress and a narrow export base.

fiscal_state.notes: Gross debt ~71% GDP with a small primary surplus achieved under the program, against crushing debt-service and chronic twin deficits - fully IMF-dependent. (IMF EFF review 2026)

monetary_independence.notes: The State Bank holds rates high but policy is IMF-conditioned and FX-crisis-prone. (SBP; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The rupee is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Collapsed from a 30%+ peak to low single digits before rebounding toward ~11% - disinflation real but fragile. (PBS; SBP)

banking_stability.notes: Heavy sovereign-debt exposure, functioning but propped by the program. (IMF Art IV)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A narrow textile-heavy export base, a widening trade deficit, tiny FDI, and heavy remittance dependence. (SBP; Finance Division)

institutional.notes: Weak rule of law under military influence, low capacity, and a heavily pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: A very low WJP standing - high impunity, military influence over the judiciary, and politicised courts. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Official data is usable but capacity-limited, with politicisation concerns. (WJP; press)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 158/180, with military surveillance and laws used against journalists. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A military-dominant hybrid with a legitimacy crisis, restricted liberties, deep cleavages, and Afghan-refugee expulsions.

political_stability.notes: A military-dominant civilian hybrid: the main opposition leader is jailed, the Feb-2024 election was widely seen as manipulated, and the coalition government carries a legitimacy crisis. (Crisis Group; Brookings)

civil_liberties.notes: A sustained opposition crackdown, protest suppression, and digital censorship. (Amnesty; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a relocation destination, with a mass expulsion of millions of Afghans underway since 2023. (UNHCR; HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep Baloch/Pashtun/sectarian cleavages and active separatism. (CFR; V-Dem)

demographics.notes: A huge, fast-growing young population straining jobs, with weak human capital. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A patron-dependent hedger, dominated by resurgent terrorism and border tensions.

alliance_reliability.notes: Active hedging - deep China/CPEC ties, a new Gulf defence pact, and warming US ties - flexible but patron-dependent. (Atlantic Council; CSIS)

conflict_involvement.notes: Resurgent terrorism (a jihadist insurgency in the northwest, a Balochistan insurgency), a tense India border including a 2025 flare-up, and Afghanistan border clashes. (UN SC; The Diplomat)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Off the FATF grey list and unsanctioned, but with persistent FX/capital-control reflexes. (IMF; FATF)

physical_practical.notes: Catastrophic climate exposure, weak healthcare, a power crisis, and very low catastrophe cover.

climate.notes: Catastrophic flood capacity (2022), extreme heat, glacier/Indus dependence, low readiness; recurrent Germanwatch CRI top-tier. (Germanwatch CRI; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: About 1% of GDP in public spend, high out-of-pocket costs, and one of the last polio-endemic countries. (WHO EMRO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: A chronic power crisis and large circular debt under restructuring. (IGC; IEEFA)

crime_safety.notes: A poor order-and-security standing, terrorism-adjacent with urban crime. (WJP)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low penetration against extreme exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A distressed economy held together by an IMF program: a heavy debt load with crushing debt-service, a narrow textile-dependent export base, and a remittance lifeline, with inflation down sharply from a peak but fragile. Institutions are weak under military influence, the Feb-2024 election left a legitimacy crisis with the main opposition leader jailed, and resurgent terrorism plus tense India and Afghanistan borders weigh heavily. Catastrophic climate exposure - the 2022 floods submerged a third of the country - a chronic power crisis, and weak healthcare round out a negative profile.
